figures.caption: Fig. 5. Reconstruction quality trends across SoLAR ablation variants. Variants incorporating GOP partitioning are marked with †.

Fig. 1. (a) Qualitative results of a long free-viewpoint video (FVV). Split-view frames compare SoLAR (left) with iFVC (right): iFVC shows visible visual deterioration over time, while SoLAR preserves fine details and high-fidelity rendering. (b) Reconstruction quality trends. Competing methods suffer severe performance decay from error propagation, whereas SoLAR mitigates this, showing robust error-resilient performance in long-horizon videos.
Fig. 4. Multi-view consistency comparison. SoLAR delivers more stable and coherent renderings across novel viewpoints.

Fig. 8. Rate-distortion performance in two scenarios. The proposed framework achieves a better rate-distortion trade-off than state-of-the-art baselines and provides flexible storage–quality adaptation.
Fig. 7. Correlation between normalized PSNR and normalized gradient statistic across a 1,000-frame sequence on the Bar dataset.
TABLE III QUANTITATIVE COMPARISON ON AVATARREX-LBN1.

TABLE I QUANTITATIVE COMPARISON ON MEETING ROOM AND N3DV DATASETS. A DASH (–) INDICATES THAT THE CORRESPONDING METRIC IS NOT REPORTED IN THE ORIGINAL WORK. SOLAR-LITE DENOTES THE LIGHTWEIGHT VERSION OF SOLAR.

Fig. 2. Overview of the SoLAR framework. (a) Dynamic Anchors. Free-Viewpoint Video is represented by dynamic anchors A across timesteps. NG decodes Gaussians from anchor position x and feature f . At timestep t, BTC predicts ∆x and ∆f from xt−1, with its lightweight parameters encoded for transmission. (b) Anchor Activation Dynamics. Nm generates a probability mask to split anchors into active A and vanished V (vanished anchors are masked out), with optimized Nm as the transmission payload. (c) Latent Discrepancy Aware Recalibration. After BTC optimization, recalibration is triggered if accumulated gradient G exceeds the threshold: NG is fine-tuned (anchor features fixed), and the updated NG is transmitted.

Fig. 6. Temporal Dynamics of Reconstruction Quality and Storage Overhead. Adjusting recalibration threshold controls the recalibration frequency. Top: temporal reconstruction quality. Bottom: temporal storage overhead.
Fig. 3. Qualitative comparison. SoLAR consistently reconstructs finer details and delivers the best visual quality across all evaluated datasets.
